texts.content_text1: Before you get to the lecture, let's warm up and have a guess:
texts.content_text100: A detainee loses all their rights once imprisoned
texts.content_text300: Detainees lose their liberty, but they retain fundamental rights such as dignity, equality, and humane treatment.
texts.content_text101: Detainees can complain if their rights are violated.
texts.content_text301: They can submit complaints to prison authorities and appeal to judicial bodies if necessary.
texts.content_text102: Prison staff are not required to explain rules to detainees.
texts.content_text302: Detainees must be clearly informed of rules, rights, and procedures in a way they understand.
texts.content_text103: A detainee can request translation if they don’t understand the language.
texts.content_text303: If there is a language barrier, detainees have the right to interpretation or translation.
texts.content_text104: Religious practices are not allowed in prison.
texts.content_text304: They have the right to be informed of decisions and to appeal them through legal procedures.
texts.content_text105: Detainees must always accept disciplinary decisions without appeal.
texts.content_text305: They have the right to be informed of decisions and to appeal them through legal procedures.
texts.content_text106: Communication with family (calls, visits, letters) is allowed.
texts.content_text306: Maintaining contact with family and legal counsel is an important right and supports well-being.
